Child neglect is one of the most common forms of child maltreatment, and it continues to be a serious problem for many children. Child neglect tremendously affects the physical development, mental development, and emotional development of a child causing long term consequences, such as poor academic achievement, depression (mood), and personality disorders. Most specifically, child mistreatment involves physical neglect, medical neglect, emotional neglect and educational/developmental neglect.

Topsoil is the upper, outermost layer of soil, usually the top 5 to 10 inches. In soil classification systems, topsoil is known as the O Horizon or A Horizon; therefore, it is the very top layer. Topsoil has the highest concentration of organic matter and microorganisms and is where most of the Earth's biological soil activity occurs. Topsoil is composed of mineral particles, organic matter, water, and air.

Cars 2 is a 2011 American 3D computer animated comedy film and is a sequel to the 2006 Cars movie and the second film in the Cars franchise. The character Doc Hudson does not appear in Cars 2 because his voice actor, Paul Newman, died from lung cancer in 2008. Information included in the Cars 2 narrative was that Doc died before the events of the film, Piston Cup was renamed in his honor, and his clinic being converted into a museum.

Sniper Ghost Warrior 3 is a tactical shooter video game developed and published by CI Games for Microsoft Windows, PlayStation 4 and Xbox One, and was released worldwide on 25 April 2017. The player takes the role of U. S. Marine Captain Jonathan "Jon" North, who, along with his brother Robert, is sent to the Russian-Ukrainian border to destroy an abandoned stockpile of Soviet-era bio-weapons before they can be stolen by terrorists. Two years later, Jon is sent to Georgia to help destabilize the local Georgian separatist cells, who are reported to be receiving an abnormally high level of funding and resources.
Avatar is a 2009 American epic science fiction film. Actor Sam Worthington played the role of Jake Sully. Jake Sully was the main human character in the film.

Lionel Jefferson is the character of the son of George and Louise Jefferson from the hit sitcoms All in the Family and The Jeffersons. He was originally portrayed by D'Urville Martin for two unaired pilots, before the role was recast with Mike Evans, who held the role from 1971-1975. He was later played by Damon Evans from 1975-1978, though Mike Evans eventually returned to the role before the end of the series. Jovan Adepo portrayed the character for the television special Live in Front of a Studio Audience: Norman Lear's All in the Family and The Jeffersons in 2019.

The iPhone 6 and iPhone 6 Plus are the eighth generation of the iPhone smartphone series. Designed and marketed by Apple Inc. , they were announced on September 9, 2014, and released on September 19, 2014. The iPhone 6's original price at launch was $649, while the iPhone 6 Plus' one was $749.

Iron Fist is an American television series created by Scott Buck for the streaming service Netflix, based on the Marvel Comics character of the same name and set in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, sharing continuity with the rest of the franchise's films. The character of Davos, played by Sacha Dhawan, makes an appearance in the ninth episode of the first season and is ultimately revealed to be the real villain of the series.

John Lennon sang lead vocals on the song, Twist and Shout, which was included on the Beatles' first studio album, Please Please Me, which was released in March 1963. Twist and Shout is also the title of the first UK extended play record by the Beatles and features lead vocals by John Lennon, Paul McCartney, and George Harrison. It was released in July 1963. The Beatles also released an album called Twist and Shout in 1964 which included songs with lead vocals by Ringo Starr, George Harrison, John Lennon, and Paul McCartney.

Letters of Administration are granted by a Surrogate Court or probate registry to appoint appropriate people to deal with a deceased person's estate where property will pass under Intestacy Rules or where there are no executors living having been validly appointed under the deceased's will. In the UK, one would get a letter of administration from the High Court of Justice Family Division. In New Brunswick, Canada, an individual would get a letter of administration from the Probate Court of New Brunswick, and in Novia Scotia, Canada, they would go to the Probate Court of Nova Scotia. In providences in Canada other than Novia Scotia or New Brunswick, one would receive it from superior courts.

The Count of Monte Cristo is an adventure novel written by French author Alexandre Dumas completed in 1844, that has spawned several adaptations. The original book takes place on takes place in France, Italy, and islands in the Mediterranean during the historical events of 1815–1839: the era of the Bourbon Restoration, while the 2002 American adaptation takes place in the island of Elba and the mainland city of Marseille, during the same time frame. Alternatively, the 1999 version called Forever Mine is mostly set in Miami in the year 1987. In 1986, an Indian take on the story was released with the movie Veta, which takes place in India in 1939.

A tendon or sinew is a tough, high-tensile-strength band of dense fibrous connective tissue that connects muscle to bone. It is able to efficiently transmit the mechanical forces of muscle contraction to the skeletal system without sacrificing its ability to withstand significant amounts of tension. Tendons are similar to ligaments; both are made of collagen. Ligaments connects bones to other bones, while tendons connect muscle to bone.

Cole is a name of Middle English origin, meaning  "swarthy, coal-black, charcoal. " The name is also an abbreviation of "Nicholas" and an Americanized spelling of the German name "Kohl," of the Dutch name "Kool," and of the Scottish and Irish name "McCool. ".

The spider in the Lord of the Rings is a demon and evil spirit. Shelob is a fictional demon in the form of a giant spider from J. R. R. Tolkien's Middle-earth legendarium. She appears at the end of the fourth book, second volume "The Two Towers", of "The Lord of the Rings". Ungoliant is a fictional character in J. R. R. Tolkien's legendarium, described as an evil spirit in the form of a spider. Tolkien's original writings say that Ungoliant was a primeval spirit of night, named Móru, who aided Melkor in his attack upon the Two Trees of Valinor, draining them of their sap after Melkor had injured them.

The draft lottery for Vietnam War started on December 1, 1969, when the Selective Service System of the United States conducted two lotteries to determine the order of call to military service in the Vietnam War for men born from January 1, 1944 to December 31, 1950. The draft was conceived to address perceived inequities in the draft system as it existed previously, and to add more military personnel towards the Vietnam War.

Saugus is a town in Essex County, Massachusetts in the Greater Boston area. The zip code for Saugus, Massachusetts is 01906. Saugus is also a neighborhood in Santa Clarita, California. The zip codes for the Saugus neighborhood in Santa Clarita, California are 91350, 91390.

The original and canonical Oz books by L. Frank Baum include 14 full-length Oz books, beginning with The Wonderful Wizard of Oz. Including compilations, there are 18 books in the Wizard of Oz. In addition to the canonical Oz books, several of Baum's works that are not Oz stories are set in the same fictional universe as the Oz books, and include character crossovers and including these works, there are 24 books in the Wizard of Oz. These compilations include: Queen Zixi of Ix, The Magical Monarch of Mo, The Sea Fairies, Sky Island, The Life and Adventures of Santa Claus, and John Dough and the Cherub.

Yale University is a private Ivy League research university in New Haven, Connecticut. New Haven had the first public tree planting program in America, producing a canopy of mature trees, including some large elms, that gave the city the nickname "The Elm City".

Creature from the Black Lagoon is a 1954 American black-and-white 3D monster horror film from Universal-International. Many of the on-water scenes were filmed at Rice Creek near Palatka, Florida. Ben Chapman portrayed the Gill-Man for the majority of the scenes shot at Universal City, California. Ricou Browning played the Gill-Man in the underwater shots, which were filmed by the second unit in Wakulla Springs, Florida. Creature From the Black Lagoon: The Musical was a live performance show formerly located at the Universal Studios Hollywood theme park in Los Angeles, California.

R. Subhash Reddy was appointed the 24th Chief Justice of Gujarat High Court on 13 February 2016. Reddy succeeded the 23rd Chief Justice Bhaskar Bhattacharya. Bhattacharya was preceded by the 22nd Chief Justice S J Mukhopadhaya.

Lord & Taylor was the oldest department store chain in America, with different CEOs over the years. Under the leadership of CEO Joseph E. Brooks during the 1970s, the company expanded aggressively. In 2005, Federated CEO Terry Lundgren acquired the department stores, creating one of the largest retailers in the world, and on 2006 Lord & Taylor would be sold again. It went into liquidation in 2020 and Saadia Group purchased its assets and revived the brand in 2021 as an online store.

A pension is a fund into which a sum of money is added during an employee's employment years and from which payments are drawn to support the person's retirement from work in the form of periodic payments. For the United States Congress, as of 2019, members who participated in the congressional pension system are vested after five years of service. A full pension is available to members 62 years of age with 5 years of service, at 50 years or older with 20 years of service, or 25 years of service at any age.

Sports Illustrated, is an American sports magazine which was first published in 1954 and is owned by Authentic Brands Group. Michael Jordan has appeared on the cover of Sports Illustrated the most, with 50 times, and on the September 1996 issue, Jordan was named the greatest athlete of the past 50 years. Australian model, actress and television host Elle MacPherson is known for her record five cover appearances for the Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Issue beginning in the 1980s, leading to her nickname "The Body", coined by Time in 1989. The team that has been on the cover of Sports Illustrated the most is the Los Angeles Lakers and the sport that has been on the cover of Sports Illustrated the most is baseball.

Undun is a popular song by Canadian rock band The Guess Who that contains the lyrics she's come undone. The song was released in 1969 with vocals sung by Burton Cummings. Numerous jazz as well as other artists have covered Undun, including Hennie Bekker, Steve Cole, The Phil Dwyer Septet and Kurt Elling.

The 2018 College Football Playoff National Championship was a college football bowl game that determined a national champion in the NCAA Division I Football Bowl Subdivision for the 2017 season. In this championship, the Alabama Crimson Tide defeated the Georgia Bulldogs 26–23, coming back from a 13–0 deficit at halftime to secure the win in overtime. The 2019 College Football Playoff National Championship was a college football bowl game that determined a national champion in the NCAA Division I Football Bowl Subdivision for the 2018 season. In this championship, which was played at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California, on January 7, 2019, the Clemson Tigers defeated the Alabama Crimson Tide by a score of 44–16 to win the championship with an undefeated 15–0 record.

The Honda Civic is a series of automobiles manufactured by Honda since 1972. The Honda Civic 5-door Liftback-Hatchback was last made in 2001. The Honda Civic 3-door Hatchback was last made in 2011. The Honda Civic 5-door Hatchback is still currently being made.

The individuals who introduced many names of genera that were later adopted in the first widely used system for naming plants an animals were Caspar Bauhin, who is known for pruning the Latin descriptions, and Gaspard Bauhin, who is known for his book Pinax theatri botanici that featured many names of genera that were later adopted by Carl Linnaeus. Carl Linnaeus is credited with the formal introduction of the first widely used system for naming plants and animals, as he was a Swedish botanist, zoologist, taxonomist, and physician who formalized binomial nomenclature, which is the modern system of naming organisms. He is known as the "father of modern taxonomy".

The Persian Calendars are a succession of calendars invented or used for over two millennia in Iran and one of the longest chronological records in human history. The year 2017 was equivalent to the year 1395 on the Persian Calendar, meaning that 2016 and 2015 were equivalent to 1394 and 1393 respectively.

The most recent Olympic closing ceremony took place on August 8, 2021 at 20:00 JST in Tokyo. Previously, the 2018 Winter Olympics closing ceremony took place on February 25, 2018 at 20:00 KST in Pyeongchang, South Korea. The 2016 Summer Olympics closing ceremony took place on August 21, 2016 from 20:00 to 22:50 BRT in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil and the 2014 Winter Olympics closing ceremony took place on February 23, 2014 from 20:14 to 22:25 MSK in Sochi, Russia.

Previously, the 2018 Winter Olympics closing ceremony took place on February 25, 2018 at 20:00 KST in Pyeongchang, South Korea. The 2016 Summer Olympics closing ceremony took place on August 21, 2016 from 20:00 to 22:50 BRT in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil and the 2014 Winter Olympics closing ceremony took place on February 23, 2014 from 20:14 to 22:25 MSK in Sochi, Russia.

"A Change Is Gonna Come" is a song by American artist Sam Cooke that initially appeared on Cooke's album "Ain't That Good News" and was later performed in a number of events. For instance, the track was sang at both the 2004 Nobel Peace Prize Concert, performed by Patti LaBelle, and Obama's inaguration, where Bettye LaVette and Jon Bon Jovi were the artists that sang it. At the 2013 Sound of Change Concert,  Beyoncé Knowles performed it, with Céline Dion doing it during a 2019 tribute to  Aretha Franklin. In 2017, "A Change is Gonna Come" was also covered by Greta Van Fleet and released on their double EP "From the Fires".

The current and 38th Leader of the Official Opposition in Ontario is Andrea Horwath of the New Democratic Party, who has held the position since 2018. Prior to Horwath, the title belonged to a series of Progressive Conservative Party leaders, including 37th Opposition Leader Vic Fedeli, 36th Opposition Leader Patrick Brown, 35th Opposition Leader Jim Wilson, and 34th Opposition Leader Tim Hudak.

might become a conditioned stimulus when used together with an unconditioned stimulus. Russian physiologist Ivan Pavlov is best-known for demonstrating that a reflex could be conditioned to a neutral stimulus. He conducted multiple experiments investigating digestion in dogs in which neutral, unconditioned, and conditioned stimuli were used. Edwin Burket Twitmyer, a professor of Psychology and director of the Psychological Laboratory and Clinic at the University of Pennsylvania, independently discovered classical conditioning at approximately the same time as the Pavlov, but this achievement is typically unrecognized.

The Fugitive is an American action drama television series created by Roy Huggins that aired on CBS from October 6, 2000 to May 25, 2001 and is a remake of the 1960s TV series of the same name, starring Tim Daly as Kimble, Mykelti Williamson as Gerard, and Stephen Lang as the one-armed man. In the 1963 series, David Janssen played the fugitive. The Fugitive is an American action thriller television series developed by Nick Santora for Quibi. It is an update on the 1963 television series and the 1993 film, both of the same name, but with new characters. The series premiered on August 3, 2020 and starred Boyd Holbrook as the fugitive.

Dante Alighieri was an Italian poet, writer and philosopher, who is described as the "father" of the Italian language, and in Italy he is often referred to as il Sommo Poeta, which means "the Supreme Poet". His Divine Comedy, originally called Comedìa and later christened Divina by Giovanni Boccaccio, is widely considered one of the most important poems of the Middle Ages and the greatest literary work in the Italian language. Dante was instrumental in establishing the literature of Italy, as his depictions of Hell, Purgatory and Heaven provided inspiration for the larger body of Western art and literature. Also, the first use of the interlocking three-line rhyme scheme, or the terza rima, is attributed to him.

Arsenal most recently won the Premier League at rival club Tottenham Hotspur's home stadium White Hart Lane on April 25, 2004. The only previous time Arsenal won the Premier League title at their rivals' home ground was on May 3, 1971.

Gluconeogenesis is a metabolic pathway that results in the generation of glucose from certain non-carbohydrate carbon substrates. In vertebrates, this process can occur in both the kidneys and the liver. However, in mammals it can also take place within a muscle, the intestine and the astrocytes of the brain. Inside the eukaryotic cell itself, all this happens in the mitochondrion and cytosol.

National Lampoon's Vacation, sometimes referred to as Vacation, is a 1983 American road comedy film directed by Harold Ramis, starring Chevy Chase, Beverly D'Angelo, Imogene Coca, Randy Quaid, John Candy, and Christie Brinkley in her acting debut. The Wagon Queen Family Truckster station wagon was created specifically for the film. It is based on a 1979 Ford LTD Country Squire station wagon.

Attack on Titan is a Japanese dark fantasy anime television series and takes place in a post-apocalyptic world. Season two continues the story. The second season of Attack on Titan was originally broadcast on MBS TV, the Japanese television station, from April 1 to June 17, 2017. An English dubbed version was aired from April 22, 2017 - July 22, 2017.

The biggest ballpark in the MLB by capacity is the Dodgers Stadium with a capacity of 56,000 people. The biggest ballpark in the MLB by field dimensions is the Houston Astros Stadium, currently called Minute Maid Park.

The Andes Mountains in Bolivia were famous for providing vast quantities of silver for Spain during the period of the New World Spanish Empirel, with Cerro Rico being one of the most popular sites. It is estimated that eighty-five percent of the silver produced in the central Andes during this time came from this particular mountain. Nonetheless, after the 1800s, primary type of mining that takes place in Bolivia is that of tin.

The Minoan language is the language  of the ancient Minoan civilization of Crete written in Cretan hieroglyphs and later in Linear A. Since the Cretan hieroglyphs are undeciphered and Linear A only partly deciphered, the Minoan language is unknown and unclassified.

As Good as It Gets is a 1997 American romantic comedy-drama film. In the movie, the main character, Melvin Udall, cares for a Griffon Bruxellois named Verdell. The dog was played by multiple actual dogs: Timer, Sprout, Debbie, Billy, Parfait, Jill.

There are different actresses in the Trivago ads based on the country the ad is shown in. In the UK, Trivago's advertisements are fronted by Australian actress and musician Gabrielle Miller. In Japan, Trivago is represented in their TV commercials by singer/songwriter Natalie Emmons, who uses the pseudonym "Strae" in the United States. In Malaysia, actress and TV host Dahlia Shazwan has starred in Trivago's advertisements.

There is a song titled "Light It Up" that was co-written and recorded by American country music singer Luke Bryan. It was released in August 2017 as the first single from his 2017 album What Makes You Country. There is also another song titled "Light It Up" by Major Lazer, an American electronic music producing group, released in 2015.

The Partial Test Ban Treaty, which prohibited all test detonations of nuclear weapons except for those conducted underground, was signed in 1963. In July 1974, the United States and Soviet Union signed the Threshold Test Ban Treaty to establish a nuclear "threshold," by prohibiting nuclear tests of devices having a yield exceeding 150 kilotons. Both of these treaties were signed in Moscow, Soviet Union. The Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty, a multilateral treaty that bans all nuclear tests for both civilian and military purposes was adopted by the United Nations General Assembly on 10 September 1996 in New York City, but has not entered into force, as eight specific nations have not ratified the treaty.

After the end of the last feudal dynasty in China, with the rise of the new cultural movement, Chinese artists began to be influenced by Western art and began to integrate Western art into Chinese culture. At the beginning of the 20th century, oil paintings were introduced to China, and more and more Chinese painters began to touch Western painting techniques and combine them with traditional Chinese painting. Meanwhile, a new form of painting, comics, has also begun to rise. It was popular with many people and became the most affordable way to entertain at the time.

The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring is a 2001 epic fantasy adventure film directed by Peter Jackson, based on the 1954 novel The Fellowship of the Ring, the first volume of J. R. R. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings. The film is the first installment in the Lord of the Rings trilogy. Two characters in the Lord of the Rings, are Gil-galad and Elendil. Gil-galad died first offscreen and Elendil was the first to die on screen.

The song "I'll Be There" that was written and originally recorded by Bobby Darin was first released as B-side to his single "Bill Bailey. " As such it entered the Billboard charts on July 11, 1960 and reached position 79. The song "I'll Be There" by the Jackson 5 was the first single released on their Third Album. It was written by Berry Gordy, Hal Davis, Bob West, and Willie Hutch and released by Motown Records on August 28, 1970.

The sixth season of America's Best Dance Crew, also known as America's Best Dance Crew: Season of the Superstars, premiered on April 7, 2011. The winning group from the the season's dance crew was I. aM. mE, which stands for inspire, motivate, and energize, an American hip-hop dance crew from Houston, Texas. The dancers names are Phillip Chbeeb, Di Moon Zhang and Brandon Harrell.

Eddy Merckx is tied with Mark Cavendish for the greatest number of stage wins ever in the Tour de France with 34. Three riders have won 8 stages in a single year: Charles Pélissier, Eddy Merckx, Freddy Maetens. Overall, the country with the most stage wins is France.

The Truth About the Harry Quebert Affair is an American mystery drama television miniseries, based on the 2014 novel of the same name by Joël Dicker, that premiered on Epix. On April 6, 2018, a "sneak peek" 35 minute presentation of select scenes from the series were debuted at international television festival Canneseries in Cannes, France. The full release date for the show was September 4, 2018.

Tomb Raider is a 2018 action adventure film directed by Roar Uthaug, that is based on the 2013 video game of the same name. The film stars Alicia Vikander as Lara Croft, who embarks on a perilous journey to her father's last-known destination, hoping to solve the mystery of his disappearance. Maisy De Freitas plays a 7 year old Lara Croft in the film and Emily Carey plays a 14 year old Lara Croft in the film. In the 2001 movie Lara Croft: Tomb Raider, Angelina Jolie plays the role of Lara Croft.

V. C. Andrews's first series of novels, The Dollanganger Series, was published between 1979 and 1986. The order of the titles in the series is: Flowers in the Attic, Petals on the Wind, If There Be Thorns, Seeds of Yesterday, Garden of Shadows, Christopher's Diary: Secrets of Foxworth, Christopher's Diary: Echoes of Dollanganger and Christopher's Diary: Secret Brother. Not including the Christopher's Diary spin-offs, the order of books in the series is: Flowers in the Attic, Petals on the Wind, If There Be Thorns, Seeds of Yesterday, and Garden of Shadows.

A Song of Ice and Fire is a series of epic fantasy novels by the American novelist and screenwriter George R. R. Martin. He began the first volume of the series, A Game of Thrones, in 1991, and it was published in 1996. In US and Canada, the series is published by Bantam Books, an American publishing house owned entirely by parent company Random House, a subsidiary of Penguin Random House. In UK and Australia the series is published by Voyager Books.

"Take Me Out to the Ball Game" is a 1908 Tin Pan Alley song by Albert Von Tilzer and John Godfrey Knauff, who was professionally known as Jack Norworth. The song has become the official anthem of North American baseball, although neither of its authors had attended a game prior to writing the song. The song's chorus is traditionally sung during the middle of the seventh inning of a baseball game. Fans are generally encouraged to sing along, and at most ballparks, the words "home team" are replaced with the home team's name.

Roe v. Wade, 410 U. S. 113, was a landmark decision of the U. S. Supreme Court in 1973 in which the Court ruled that the Constitution of the United States protects a pregnant woman's liberty to choose to have an abortion without excessive government restriction. Roe v Wade was challenged last in the case of Whole Woman's Health v. Hellerstedt, where the Supreme Court in a 5–3 decision on June 27, 2016, swept away forms of state restrictions on the way abortion clinics can function. The Texas legislature enacted in 2013 restrictions on the delivery of abortions services that created an undue burden for women seeking an abortion by requiring abortion doctors to have difficult to obtain "admitting privileges" at a local hospital and by requiring clinics to have costly hospital grade facilities.

The current population of the United Kingdom is 67. 08 million. It is the 21st most populated country in the world and has a population density of 700 people per square mile. The population of the UK in 2011 was 63,182,000, of whom 31 million were male and 32 million female according to the 2011 census. In 2001, the population of the UK was reported as 59,113,000 and in 1991, the population was 57,439,000.

Organized primary option markets originated in London while organized secondary option markets originated in Chicago. The Chicago Board of Trade established the Chicago Board Options Exchange in 1973. This was the first exchange to list standardized, exchange-traded stock options, and it began its first day of trading on April 26, 1973, in celebration of the 125th birthday of the Chicago Board of Trade.

Stefano DiMera is a fictional character from the NBC soap opera Days of Our Lives, who was introduced on January 18, 1982 as the father of Tony DiMera, looking to expand his criminal empire to Salem. American musician and dramatic actor Joseph Mascolo played the character of Stephano from 1982-1988 and 1993-2016. Actor Frank Fata appeared in the role of Stefano for two episodes which aired on December 3 and 4, 1991 and Davide Shiavone played the role in 2007. Although the character was shot and killed in 2012, in 2019, "Stefano" returned to Salem, with the assistance of "Princess Gina" and Dr. Rolf, who transferred Stefano's "essence" - his memories, beliefs, and personality traits - into a computer chip which he has since implanted in character Steve Johnson's neck, effectively turning Steve into "the new Stefano," who has been played by Stephen Nichols from 2019-present.

Ja'net DuBois and Jeff Barry co-wrote "The Jeffersons" theme song, "Movin' on Up", which was sung by DuBois with a gospel choir. Sammy Davis, Jr. recorded a cover version of the song in 1978. DuBois is best known for her portrayal of Willona Woods, the neighborhood gossip maven and a friend of the Evans family on the CBS sitcom Good Times.

Supernatural is the title of a British anthology television series that was produced by the BBC in 1977. The series consisted of eight episodes and was broadcast on BBC1. The last three episodes in the series aired on 6 August 1977, 30 July 1977 and 16 July 1977. Supernatural is also the title of an American dark fantasy drama television series created by Eric Kripke, which first aired on September 13, 2005. The thirteenth season of this series premiered on October 12, 2017. Episodes from season thirteen also aired on November 23, 2017, November 30, 2017, and December 7, 2017. Kripke's Supernatural series concluded on November 19, 2020 with 327 episodes aired.

"If I Were a Rich Man" is a show tune from the 1964 musical Fiddler on the Roof. The lyrics were written by Sheldon Harnick and the music was composed by Jerry Bock. The song is performed by Tevye, the main character in the musical, and reflects his dreams of glory.

The Men's high jump athletics events for the 2016 Summer Paralympics took place at the Rio Olympic Stadium from September 8 to September 17, 2016. A total of three events were contested for three different classifications. Indian Paralympic high jumper Mariyappan Thangavelu won the gold medal in the F42 men's high jump. Polish athlete Maciej Lepiato, who was born with a disability affecting his left leg, won the gold medal in the F44 men's high jump. American Roderick Townsend-Roberts won the gold medal in the F47 men's high jump.

In 2017, Rafael Nadal won the US Open Men's Singles title, and Sloane Stephens won the US Open Women's Singles title. This was the 16th Major title and 3rd US Open title for Nadal. It was Stephens' first Grand Slam title. Molla Mallory holds the record for most US Open Singles titles with eight.

"Drink You Away" is a song recorded by American singer and songwriter Justin Timberlake for his fourth studio album in 2013. Country singer David Fanning released a cover of the song as his solo debut single in 2014. At the Country Music Association Awards in 2015, Justin Timberlake and Chris Stapleton performed a rendition of the song together.

John Deere is the brand name of the American corporation Deere & Company. Since 1912, the company started manufacturing in Waterloo, Iowa, where it continues to manufacture a large percentage of its tractors, namely the 7R, 8R, and 9R series. The majority of John Deere tractors made in North America are manufactured in Grovetown, Georgia, Monterrey, Mexico, and Waterloo, Iowa. In the last months of 1958, John Deere installed a factory in Rosario, Argentina. Other important global factories are Granadero Baigorria, Pune, India, Dewas, India, Mannheim, Germany, Catalão, Brazil.

Several tests can be used to assess carnial nerve vi, or the abducens nerve, that controls movement of the lateral rectus muscle, responsible for outward gaze. Ocular movements are tested by following a target with the eyes. The optokinetic nystagmus test can also be used. This involves looking at a strip of vertical lines. Additionally, extraocular movements are tested by inspecting the eyes and measuring the pupil. Pupillary light reflexes are also tested using a pen light.

"Stacy's Mom" is a pop rock song by American rock band Fountains of Wayne. It was first released on radio on May 20, 2003 and then as the lead single of the album "Welcome Interstate Managers" on September 29, 2003. The song also became available for digital download on September 29, 2003 in the US and on October 7, 2003 in the UK, followed by its commercialization in the 7" format on March 8, 2004. Years later, the American rock band Bowling for Soup covered the song and released their version on October 10, 2011.

Jenny Wolek is a fictional character from the American soap opera One Life to Live. The distant cousin of original characters Larry, Anna, and Vince Wolek, Jenny debuted June 1975, appearing continually until May 1986. The character was originally played by Katherine Glass from 1975 through 1978. She was then played by Brynn Thayer from 1978 to 1986.

Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his famous "I Have a Dream" speech during the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom on August 28, 1963, in which he called for civil and economic rights and an end to racism in the United States. Delivered to over 250,000 civil rights supporters from the steps of the Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D. C. , the speech was a defining moment of the civil rights movement and among the most iconic speeches in American history.

Michael Jackson's disco and funk song "Rock with You" was released on November 3, 1979 as the second single from Jackson's fifth solo studio album "Off the Wall". It was also the third number-one hit of the 1980s, and was re-released as a single on February 27, 2006 as part of the Visionary: The Video Singles box set.

Collingwwood and St Kilda faced during the 2010 AFL Grand Final in a series of two matches held at the Melbourne Cricket Ground. Although the premiership is usually decided by a single match, as the first grand final ended in a draw, a grand final replay was carried out the following week and was won by Collingwood. The teams also faced each other in the 1996 VFL Grand Final in a match that ended with a victory for St Kilda.

Two hurricanes named Irene have hit the United States, one in 1999 and another in 2011. In 1999, Hurricane Irene struck Florida on October 15 as a Category 1 hurricane, first at Key West and later near Cape Sable. In 2011, Hurricane Irene made landfall on the Outer Banks of North Carolina on August 27 as a Category 1 hurricane. Later that day, the storm re-emerged into the Atlantic from southeastern Virginia. Although Irene remained a hurricane over water, it weakened to a tropical storm while making yet another landfall in the Little Egg Inlet in southeastern New Jersey on August 27. A few hours later, Irene made its ninth and final landfall in Brooklyn, New York City. Early on August 29, Irene transitioned into an extratropical cyclone while striking Vermont. Before making landfall in North Carolina, Irene had also passed near the island of Saint Croix in the US Virgin Islands on August 22, 2011. The name Irene was retired in the North Atlantic after the 2011 hurricane season.
The player that holds the record of most goals in a single AFL game is Fred Fanning. Alternatively, the record of most goals kicked on their debut match in the AFL is held by John Coleman.

Both the one-cent and two-cent coins were introduced in 1966 during the decimalisation of Australian currency. They were the two lower denomination coins in the country until they stopped circulating in 1992, although none had been minted for circulation since 1990.

Bradley Stryker played Ryan's brother, Trey Atwood, in the first season of the OC, including when Ryan and Trey are caught attempting to steal a car. Logan Marshall-Green then played Trey in seasons 2 and 3 of the series when Trey is released from jail.

The Dark Crystal is a dark fantasy film directed by Jim Henson and Frank Oz that follows Jen, a Gelfling on a quest to restore balance to the world of Thra and overthrow the ruling Skeksis by restoring a powerful broken Crystal. It was filmed in between April–December 1981 and first released on December 13, 1982 in New York City, followed by its release the rest of the United States on December 17, 1982. The movie didn't arrive to the UK until February 17, 1983.

Pawley's Island in Georgetown County, South Carolina, has an official population of 103 as of the 2010 census, a significant decrease from the population of 138 reported in the 2000 census. The population was estimated to be 105 in 2015 and 106 in 2016 and 2017.

Several different voice actors have supplied Spider Man's voice in the many animated series that have been created over the years. Spider Man was voiced by Shinji Todo in the Japanese TV series, and by Neil Patrick Harris in Spider Man: The New Animated Series. Other actors who voiced the superhero in a Spider Man television series include Paul Soles in 1967, Ted Schwartz in 1981, Chris Barnes in 1994, and Robbie Daymond 2017.

General of the Army, Douglas MacArthur was an American general and field marshal of the Philippine Army. MacArthur was recalled to active duty in 1941 as commander of U. S. Army Forces in the Far East. As the senior field commander of Filipino and US forces under General Douglas MacArthur, Jonathan M. Wainwright was responsible for resisting the Japanese invasion of the Philippines, which began in December 1941.

Inspector George Gently, which is also called George Gently, is a British television crime drama series. Episode 23 was shown on 20 May 2015. The next episode, number 24, was shown about two years later, on 21 May 2017. The airing date for episode 25 is TBA, or To Be Announced.

There is more than one movie called Some Like It Hot. The 1959 film, Some Like It Hot, is an American romantic comedy starring Marilyn Monroe, Tony Curtis, and Jack Lemmon. Many of the scenes were filmed at Hotel del Coronado in San Diego, California. The 2016 film, Some Like It Hot, is a Chinese romantic comedy that was filmed in China. It won the Outstanding Film Contribution Award at the Hengdian Film and TV Festival of China.

The Price Is Right is an American television game revolving around contestants competing by identifying accurate pricing of merchandise to win cash and prizes. Contestants are selected from the studio audience when the announcer states the show's famous catchphrase, "Come on down!" The program premiered on September 4, 1972, on CBS The Price Is Right and has aired over 9,000 episodes since its debut with new seasons premiering in September each year, for example September, 2015, September, 2016, September, 2017 and it's 50th season recently airing on September 13, 2021.

The Price Is Right is an American television game revolving around contestants competing by identifying accurate pricing of merchandise to win cash and prizes. Contestants are selected from the studio audience when the announcer states the show's famous catchphrase, "Come on down!" The program premiered on September 4, 1972, on CBS The Price Is Right and has aired over 9,000 episodes since its debut with new seasons premiering in September each year, for example September, 2015, September, 2016, September, 2017 and it's 50th season recently airing on September 13, 2021.

Otto Frederick Warmbier was an American college student who entered North Korea on December 29, 2015 as part of a guided tour group with Young Pioneer Tours, a China-based budget tour operator. He was arrested on January 2, 2016 and convicted for attempting to steal a propaganda poster from his hotel, for which he was sentenced to 15 years of imprisonment with hard labor. He was released from North Korea in June 2017, in a vegetative state and died soon after.

Green, Green Grass of Home was first recorded by singer Johnny Darrell in 1965. This country song was then made popular by Porter Wagoner, host of the syndicated program The Porter Wagoner Show, the same year, when Porter's recording of the song reached number 4 on the Country chart. Then, in 1966, Tom Jones recorded and released the song in the UK, where it reached number 1 that December.

The twelfth season of the American animated television series Bob's Burgers will debut on Fox on September 26, 2021. However, the feature film based on this series that was set to be released on July 17, 2020 been postponed to May 27, 2022 due to the effects of the COVID-19 pandemic.

Elsa of Arendelle is a fictional character who appears in Walt Disney Animation Studios' 53rd animated film Frozen (2013) and its sequel Frozen II (2019). She is voiced mainly by Broadway actress and singer Idina Menzel, with Eva Bella as a young child and by Spencer Ganus as a teenager in Frozen. In Frozen II, young Elsa is voiced by Mattea Conforti (at the start of the film) and Eva Bella (archive audio). Dutch singer and actress Willemijn Verkaik dubbed Elsa in Dutch (both speaking and singing) and German (singing only), Spanish singer Gisela sang both for the Castilian Spanish and Catalan versions and French singer Anaïs Delva's singing lines were also used in the Canadian French version. In the Latin American Spanish version of Frozen, the singing voice of Elsa was by Carmen Sarahí.

In the Latin American Spanish version of Frozen, the singing voice of Elsa was by Carmen Sarahí.

Andy Murray has won two Grand Slam titles at Wimbledon. In 2013, Andy Murray defeated Novak Djokovic in the final to win the Wimbledon Gentlemen's Singles tennis title. Then, in 2016, Andy Murray defeated Milos Raonic in the final to win the Wimbledon Gentlemen's Singles tennis title. Following his win in 2013, Murray became the first British man to win the Wimbledon Gentlemen's Singles title since 1936, ending a 76-year drought.

The Ottoman Empire was one of the Central Powers fighting against the Allies, also known as the Entente Powers, in World War I. The Ottoman Empire entered the war by carrying out a surprise attack on Russia's Black Sea coast on 29 October 1914, with Russia responding by declaring war on 5 November 1914. Then, during World War I, Ottoman forces fought the Entente Powers in both the Balkans and the Middle Eastern theatres. In the Middle Eastern theatre, the Ottoman Empire was fighting the British (with the help of Jews, Greeks, Assyrians and the majority of the Arabs, along with Indians under its empire), the Russians (with the help of Armenians) and the French. Additionally, during World War I, the Ottoman Empire engaged in a genocide against local ethnicities in its own territory. The Armenian genocide, also known as the Armenian Holocaust, was the Ottoman government's systematic extermination of 1. 5 million Christian Armenians that was carried out during and after World War I and implemented in two phases.

The Stratosphere in Las Vegas officially opened on April 30, 1996; however, more than 8,000 VIP guests visited the resort for a premiere party on the night of April 29, 1996, hours before its midnight opening. Media from around the world also attended this opening event, which was broadcast live by CNBC as well as television stations in Las Vegas and Los Angeles. The Stratosphere's SkyJump, a controlled-descent, Bungee-jumping-like ride that allows riders to plummet 855 feet attached to a high-speed descent wire, opened on April 20, 2010. Insanity, the third highest thrill ride in the world, opened at The Stratosphere in 2005. Insanity dangles riders over the edge of the tower and then spins in a circular pattern at approximately forty miles per hour. In an incident shortly after opening, riders were left dangling several hundred feet for nearly an hour and a half when Insanity shut down.

The Dark Knight Rises is a 2012 superhero film directed by Christopher Nolan, who co-wrote the screenplay with his brother Jonathan Nolan, and the story with David S. Goyer. Based on the DC Comics character Batman, it is the final installment in Nolan's The Dark Knight Trilogy, and the sequel to The Dark Knight (2008). The film primarily takes place in Gotham City in the U. S. state of New Jersey. The secondary scenes with Bane takes place in Uzbekistan. The part of the film where Alfred is vacationing takes place in Florence.
Coal became a major energy source globally during the time period of the 19th and 20th centuries, while it first started to become a major energy source in the 18th century.

The 2012 United States presidential election was the 57th quadrennial presidential election, held on Tuesday, November 6, 2012. Obama defeated Romney, winning a majority of both the Electoral College and the popular vote. Obama won 332 electoral votes and 51. 1% of the popular vote compared to Romney's 206 electoral votes and 47. 2%. In the 2012 presidential race, Barack Obama won the popular vote in Nevada, Virginia, Florida, Kentucky, and Iowa.

Choo Choo Soul, a children's entertainment act consisting of singers Genevieve Goings and her partner Constantine "DC" Abramson, sang "all aboard the choo choo train" on Disney.

The Land Before Time is an American children's franchise of animated adventure films that began in 1988 with the eponymous The Land Before Time and was followed by 13 direct-to-video musical sequels, a TV series, video games, soundtracks, and related merchandising. The voice of Ducky in The Land Before Time I was provided by Judith Barsi, who was tragically killed, along with her mother, by her abusive father the year the film was released. Heather Hogan voiced the character of Ducky in The Land Before Time II-IV. American actress Aria Noelle Curzon, who received 3 Young Artist of Hollywood Awards, 2 CARE Awards, and one CLIO Award, voiced the role of Ducky in The Land Before Time video series, V-XIV.

The June 1962 Alcatraz escape was a prison break from Alcatraz Federal Penitentiary, a maximum-security facility located on Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay. Of the 36 inmates who staged 14 escape attempts over the 29 years that Alcatraz served as a federal penitentiary, twenty-three were recaptured, six were shot and killed, two drowned, and five (Frank Morris, Clarence Anglin, John Aglin, Theodore Cole, and Ralph Roe) are listed as "missing and presumed drowned". Frank Morris and brothers John and Clarence Anglin escaped in June 1962 and Theodore Cole and Ralph Roe escaped in June 1937.

There have been many Prime Ministers of Iraq. Ibrahim al-Jaafari was the first post-war Prime Minister of Iraq from 2005 to 2006. Nouri Kamil Mohammed Hasan al-Maliki, also known as Jawad al-Maliki, is an Iraqi politician who was Prime Minister of Iraq from 2006 to 2014. Haider al-Abadi is an Iraqi-British politician who was Prime Minister of Iraq from September 2014 until October 2018.

In a eukaryotic cell, chromosome replication begins on the G1 phase. In eukaryotes, the vast majority of DNA synthesis occurs during S phase of the cell cycle, and the entire genome must be unwound and duplicated to form two daughter copies. Any damaged DNA or replication errors are corrected in the G2 phase. One copy of the genome is segregated to each daughter cell at mitosis or M phase.

The Perks of Being a Wallflower is a young adult coming-of-age epistolary novel by American writer Stephen Chbosky, which was first published on February 1, 1999. Set in the early 1990s, the novel follows Charlie, an introverted observing teenager, through his freshman year of high school in a Pittsburgh suburb. The novel details Charlie's unconventional style of thinking as he navigates between the worlds of adolescence and adulthood, and attempts to deal with poignant questions spurred by his interactions with both his friends and family. Stephen Chbosky wrote and directed a film based on the novel in 2012, in which the story was set and filmed in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.

Jamaica is located in the Greater Antilles, 145 kilometres south of Cuba, and 191 kilometres west of Hispaniola.

The song, Nights in White Satin, by the Moody Blues, has been featured in many movies including a 1987 film entitled Nights in White Satin. In 1991, it was included in the psychological thriller film, Shattered. In 1992, it was featured in a science-fiction horror film called Split Second. And in 2012, the song was featured in the soundtrack of the fantasy horror comedy film, Dark Shadows along with several 1970s rock and pop songs.

The cumberland river's headwater begins as three separate forks that begin in Kentucky. Martin's Fork, Clover Fork, and Poor Fork, are the three forks that all merge in Baxter, KY. It ends at the Ohio River near Paducah, Kentucky, and the mouth of the Tennessee River.
Joy Fishwick is who John Stape killed by manslaughter in Coronation St. and Charlotte Hoyle is his murder victim.

The uppermost layer of the skin is called the epidermis, which is made up of flattened cells that overlie a base layer composed of columnar cells. The epidermis layer provides a barrier to infection from environmental pathogens and regulates the amount of water released from the body into the atmosphere through transepidermal water loss.

Nanny and the Professor is an early 1970's American sitcom about a magical British nanny taking care of a family in need of guidance. In 1972, a TV movie was made based on the sitcom using the same name and original cast. Kim Richards played Prudence, the youngest child, in both versions.

"I Can't Make You Love Me" is a song written by Mike Reid and Allen Shamblin that was first recorded by American singer Bonnie Raitt with Bruce Hornsby on the piano in 1991. Additional versions of "I Can't Make You Love Me" have been recorded by George Michael in 1997, Boyz II Men in 2009, Adele in 2011 and Priyanka Chopra in 2014.

Great Bear Lake, which is located in the boreal forest of Canada, is the largest lake entirely in Canada, the fourth-largest in North America, and the eighth-largest in the world. Lake Superior and Lake Huron are larger but straddle the Canada–US border. Lake Superior is the largest and northernmost of the Great Lakes of North America, and among freshwater lakes, it is the world's largest by surface area and the third-largest by volume.

The Hobbit is a film series consisting of three high fantasy adventure films directed by Peter Jackson which are titled The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey, The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug, and The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies. The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey's world premier was at the Embassy Theatre in Wellingon, New Zealand on 28 November 2012, with the film's wide release in New Zealand on 12 December 2012. The film was released 13 December 2012 in Europe, 14 December 2012 in India, Canada and United States, and 26 December 2012 in Australia. It was also screened at the 65th Royal Film Performance in London on 12 December 2012.

Early colonists in Virginia and the Carolinas may have grown potatoes from seeds or tubers from Spanish ships, but the earliest certain potato crop in North America was in Londonderry, New Hampshire in 1719. The plants were from Ireland, so the crop became known as the "Irish potato". Thomas Jefferson says of the white potato, "You say the potato is a native of the US. I presume you speak of the Irish potato. I have enquired much into this question, and think I can assure you that plant is not a native of North America. It came from Ireland. ".

Like a Lion is song written by Daniel Bashta that was originally performed by Passion with David Crowder on the 2010 album Passion: Awakening. In 2011, this song was covered by Newsboys as God's Not Dead (Like a Lion) and released as a single from their album God's Not Dead. In the Newsboys' version, the lead vocals are performed by Michael Tait and Kevin Max is featured. The Newsboys' version charted in 2014 after the release of the film God's Not Dead. The band performs the song in a concert sequence at the end of the film.

The Louisville Cardinals have both men's and women's basketball teams representing the University of Louisville in the Atlantic Coast Conference of NCAA Division I. The men's team has won 3 national basketball championships; one in 1980, one in 1986 and a third in 2013 which was vacated. The Louisville Cardinals women's basketball team has won 0 championships.

Wile E. Coyote and the Road Runner are a duo of cartoon characters from the Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies series of animated cartoons. In each episode, the cunning, devious and constantly hungry Coyote repeatedly attempts to catch the Road Runner, but is successful only on extremely rare occasions. The Coyote's name of Wile E. is a pun of the word "wily. " The "E" stands for "Ethelbert" according to an issue of a Looney Tunes comic book.

"Running Scared" was the first number one hit for Roy Orbison in the US Hot 100 and Canada. "Only the Lonely (Know the Way I Feel)" was the first number one hit for Roy Orbison in the UK and Ireland. Orbison wrote or co-wrote almost all of his own top hits, including "Only the Lonely (Know the Way I Feel)" and "Running Scared. ".

Walnut Grove is a city in Redwood County, Minnesota, in the United States. At the 2000 census, the city had 599 people, 291 households, and 178 families. Between 2001 and 2006 Walnut Grove gained more than 250 residents. As of the 2010 census, the population had grown to 871 people, 313 households, and 210 families.

The first explorer to set foot on Mount Everest was George Mallory, on September 23, 1921 as part of a British expedition under the leadership of Colonel Charles Howard-Bury. The Howard-Bury expedition made it as far as the North Col before they were forced to withdraw. It wasn't until 29 May 1953 that New Zealand explorer Sir Edmund Percival Hillary and Nepalese Sherpa Tenzing Norgay would become the first people to summit Everest as part of the ninth British expedition to the mountain, led by John Hunt.

Will Horton from the TV show "Days of Our Lives" was initially played by a series of child actors and then, as he grew, adult actors. Child actors Shawn and Taylor Carpenter portrayed the character from 1995-2002. Then Darian Weiss (2002-2003), Christopher Gerse (2003-2008), and Dylan Patton (2009-2010). Then he was portrayed by adult actors Chandler Massey, who played the character from 2010-2014 and 2017 to the present day, and Guy Wilson, who played the character from 2014-2015.

Which is the smallest continent of the world in terms of area other than Australia depends on the model used. Using the seven-continent model, which is usually taught in most English-speaking countries, it's Europe. Using the six-continent model, in which Europe and Asia are combined, it's Antarctica. The six-continent combined-Eurasia model is mostly used in Russia, Eastern Europe, and Japan.

The French had many mottos, or slogans, during the time of the French Revolution with the popularly accepted slogan for the Republic being liberty, equality, fraternity. The slogan that came from Masonic lodges is Union, Strength, Virtue. Another slogan was The Nation, The Law, The King. Unity, indivisibility of the Republic; liberty, equality, brotherhood or death was a slogan that was used in the context of Federalist revolts and foreign invasion.

In World War 2, the USA fought both a group of nations called The Axis powers and the countries of Germany, Japan, and Italy, which the Tripartite Pact united as the Axis powers in September 1940. The Axis expanded in November 1940 when Hungary, Slovakia, and Romania joined.

Shetland is a Scottish crime drama television series made by ITV Studios for BBC Scotland and first broadcast on 10 March 2013. The series was initially based upon the novels of Ann Cleeves, but the series was brought to screen by David Kane, who has remained the principal writer throughout the series. Series 1 of Shetland had 2 episodes, while series 4 had 6.

Pride and Prejudice is an 1813 romantic novel of manners written by Jane Austen. Jane Austen wrote the book between October 1796 and August 1797, and it was published on 28 January 1813. Though it is mainly called a romantic novel, it can also be considered a satirical book.

Fulham Football Club is an English professional association football club based in Fulham, London. Founded in 1879, Fulham is London's oldest professionally playing football club. Fulham played at Wembley Stadium during the 1975 FA Cup Final which took place on 3 May 1975. The team also played in the 2018 EFL Championship play-off Final at Wembley Stadium where they defeated Aston Villa.

The Smart Fortwo is a rear-engine, rear-wheel-drive, 2-passenger hatchback microcar manufactured and marketed by the Smart division of the German multinational Daimler AG. Mitsubishi makes the engine for the normally aspirated and turbo versions of the smart car. Brabus makes the high-powered engine for the smart car.

The Isle of Man has had several capitals. The town of Douglas is its current capital, while its capital was the town of Castletown until 1869, the year Douglas became the home of the island's parliament, Tynwald, and therefore the capital.

"Look What You Made Me Do" is the lead single from American singer-songwriter Taylor Swift's sixth studio album, Reputation. The song was released on August 24, 2017, and was produced by Jack Antonoff and Taylor Swift. The songwriters for the single include Jack Antonoff, Rob Manzoli, Richard Fairbrass, Taylor Swift, and Fred Fairbrass.

The National Youth Service Corps, or NYSC, is a program set up by the Nigerian government to involve Nigerian graduates in nation building and the development of the country. To be eligible to participate in the compulsory one year service, a graduate must be below the age of 30 years upon graduation, or else they will be given a Certificate of Exemption, which is also equivalent to the NYSC Discharge Certificate. A graduate who graduated before 30 years but skipped the service year, will still be eligible to serve even if he wants at the age of 60, since his certificate of graduation was dated before he clocked 30 years of age. Graduates of the country can't request for exemption by themselves, unless they are disabled, have served in the military or paramilitary for a period of more than one year or have exceeded the age of 30 years when they graduated and part-time graduates are given an Exemption Letter, since they're not allowed to serve.

Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego? is the soundtrack to the game show of the same name that ran from 1991 to 1995. Rockapella is an American a cappella musical group formed in 1986 in New York City. In the 1990s, they are best remembered for their role as a vocal house band and resident comedy troupe on the PBS children's geography game show "Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego?", based on the classic computer game of the same name developed and published by Broderbund. On the album, Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego?, Rockapella, Urban Blight, The Persuasions, Tito Puente, Brian Dewan, Eileen Ivers, and the 3 Brave Woodsmen all sing.

Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego? is the soundtrack to the game show of the same name that ran from 1991 to 1995. At the end of Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows, following Voldemort's death, Kingsley Shacklebolt takes over as the Minster for Magic, changing the ministry for the better. George Harris is the actor who plays Kingsley in the Harry Potter movies Order of the Phoenix and Deathly Hallows Part 1 and Part 2. By the time of Harry Potter and the Cursed Child, Hermione Granger is the Minister for Magic. However, in the beginning of the Harry Potter series, Cornelius Oswald Fudge is first mentioned in Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone as the Minister for Magic of the United Kingdom. Fudge was portrayed by Robert Hardy in the film series. Then, Rufus Scrimgeour serves as the Minister for Magic of the United Kingdom from Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince until his death, succeeding Cornelius Fudge who was ousted by the wizarding community for his failure to announce the return of Voldemort. Bill Nighy is the actor who plays Scrimgeour in Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows – Part 1. Pius Thicknesse is first introduced in Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows. He is the Head of the Department of Magical Law Enforcement at the start of the book. After the coup in which Scrimgeour is killed, the Ministry comes under the de facto control of Voldemort, who appoints Thicknesse as his puppet Minister. Guy Henry plays Thicknesse in Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows – Part 1 and Part 2, in which he is depicted as a Death Eater even before Scrimgeour's death. In Part 2, Thicknesse is killed by Voldemort.

There are several events title "E3 2019". The Electronic Entertainment Expo 2019, E3 2019, was the 25th Electronic Entertainment Expo, during which hardware manufacturers, software developers, and publishers from the video game industry presented new and upcoming products to the attendees, primarily retailers and members of the video game press. The event, organized by the Entertainment Software Association (ESA), took place at the Los Angeles Convention Center from June 11–13, 2019. The 2019 E3 BinckBank Classic is a road cycling one-day race, scheduled to be held on 29 March 2019 in Belgium.

Happy Feet is a 2006 computer-animated musical comedy film directed, produced, and co-written by George Miller and has a 2011 sequel called Happy Feet Two. In the film, every emperor penguin sings a unique song to attract a mate, but the character of Mumble cannot sing, only tap dance. In the film, the voice of baby Mumble is acted out by American actress and singer E. G. Daily, who also voiced Tommy in Rugrats, Buttercup in Powerpuff Girls, and Bamm-Bamm Rubble in the Flintstones. The voice of adult Mumble, in both the original movie and its sequel, is performed by American actor Elijah Wood, who is best known for his role in the Lord of the Rings trilogy.

One Day at a Time is an American television sitcom that aired on CBS from December 16, 1975, to May 28, 1984. American actor and television and film director Michael Lembeck played Max on the series. The 2017 series, One Day at a Time, is an American sitcom based on the 1975 series of the same title. Arthur Edward Quinn, an American actor, model and musician, played Max on the 2017 series.

In the United States, 30 states have a coastline: 23 with a coastline on the Arctic Ocean, Atlantic Ocean (including the Gulf of Mexico), and/or Pacific Ocean, and eight with a Great Lakes shoreline. In the continental US, Alaska has the most coastline. In the contiguous US, Florida has the most coastline.

Season 3 Episode 11 of Little House on the Prairie, titled Blizzard, originally aired on Januare 3, 1977. The Little House on the Prairie television series was based on The Little House Books by Laura Ingles Wilder. One of the books in the Little House series, The Long Winter, is an autobiographical children's novel that is set in the southeastern Dakota Territory during the severe winter of 1880 to1881, when Wilder turned 14 years old.

The Constitution of India is the supreme law of India. The principal preparer of the first draft of the constitution of India was Bhimrao Ramji Ambedkar, an Indian jurist, economist, politician and social reformer, who inspired the Dalit Buddhist movement and campaigned against social discrimination towards the untouchables (Dalits). A revised draft constitution was prepared by the committee and submitted to the assembly on 4 November 1947. Dr B. R. Ambedkar in his concluding speech in Constituent Assembly on November 25, 1949.

The women's curling tournament of the 2014 Winter Olympics was held at the Ice Cube Curling Center in Sochi, Russia on 10–20 February 2014. Canada won the gold medal with a team consisting of Jennifer Jones, Kaitlyn Lawes, Jill Officer, Dawn McEwen, and Kirsten Wall. Sweden won the silver medal, with a team consisting of Maria Prytz, Christina Bertrup, Maria Wennerström, Margaretha Sigfridsson, and Agnes Knochenhauer. Great Britain won the bronze medal, with a team consisting of Eve Muirhead, Anna Sloan, Vicki Adams, Claire Hamilton, and Lauren Gray.

The Kokoda Track Campaign began on 21 July 1942 when Japanese forces landed and established beachheads near Gona and Buna. The campaign ended on 16 November 1942 when two brigades of the Australian 7th Division crossed the Kumusi River at Wairopi, and advanced on the Japanese beachheads in a joint Australian and United States operation.

The fourth season of the American television series The Flash, which is based on the DC Comics character Barry Allen / Flash, premiered on The CW on October 10, 2017, and ran for 23 episodes until May 22, 2018. Episode 9 of Season 4 came out on December 5, 2017. The season was made available for streaming on Netflix in late May 2018, soon after the season finale aired. It was released on Blu-ray on August 28, 2018.

James Carter Pankow is an American trombone player, songwriter and brass instrument player, best known as a founding member of the rock band Chicago. He was born on August 20, 1947, making him 68 years old at the end of 2015, 69 at the end of 2016 and 70 years old at the end of 2017.

Davy Jones is a fictional character and is one of the main antagonists of the Pirates of the Caribbean film series. He is portrayed by Bill Nighy. The character is voiced by English actor Robin Atkin Downes.

"It's Such a Pretty World Today" is the title of a popular song released in 1967. The song was originally a country music single by singer Wynn Stewart. That same year, Mexican-American singer Andy Russell recorded a cover version of the song. Other artists who have recorded cover versions of the song include: Loretta Lynn, Nancy Sinatra, and Connie Smith all in 1967; the Jimmy Bowen Singers made a non-charting version in 1967; and Marie Osmond in 1973 on her "Paper Roses" album.

The Australian Grand Prix, is an annual motor racing event, which is currently under contract to host Formula One until 2025. Lewis Hamilton, a British racing driver, won the 2015 Australian Grand Prix. The reigning World Champion Nico Rosberg was the winner of the 2016 Australian Grand Prix, but he did not defend his win as he retired from the sport after the 2016 season. Sebastian Vettel won the 2017 Australian Grand Prix, which was his and Ferrari's first win since the 2015 Singapore Grand Prix.

"None of the Above" (or NOTA) has been provided as an option to the voters of India in most elections since 2009. The specific symbol for NOTA in India, a ballot paper with a black cross across it, was introduced on 18 September 2015.

In the late 1980s Apple was a pioneer of an accounting technique, "Double Irish With a Dutch Sandwich", which reduces taxes by routing profits through Irish subsidiaries and the Netherlands and then to the Caribbean. On August 30, 2016, after two-year "EU illegal State aid case against Apple in Ireland", the EU's competition commissioner, Margrethe Vestager, concluded that Apple had received "illegal state aid" from Ireland during 2004–2014 via its use of a hybrid "Double Irish" tax scheme on which it received private rulings by the Irish Revenue Commissioners. The Commission ordered Apple to pay 13 billion euros or $14. 5 billion, plus interest, in unpaid taxes, the largest corporate tax fine in history. During the 2000's in the United States, Senators Carl Levin and John McCain led an investigation into Apple's accounting practices, as well.

2010 reggae and pop-rap song "Billionaire" is performed by Travie McCoy featuring Bruno Mars. The song was co-written by Travie McCoy, Bruno Mars, Philip Lawrence, and Ari Levine and showcases lead vocalists Mars and McCoy singing lyrics written by Mars and Levine.

VY Canis Majoris is an extreme oxygen-rich (O-rich) red hypergiant (RHG) or red supergiant (RSG) and pulsating variable star 1. 2 kiloparsecs, 3,900 light-years, from the solar system in the slightly southern constellation of Canis Major. The generally accepted temperature of Vy Canis Majoris is 3,490±90 K. According to a 2006 study, the temperature of Vy Canis Majoris is 3,650 K. according to a study which derived a luminosity of 430,000 L, the temperature of Vy Canis Majoris is 3,450–3,535 K.

In the 1914 film "Samson", George Periolat played Manoah, Samson's father, and actress Lule Warrenton played Manoah's wife. In the 2018 film "Samson", Rutger Haur played Samson's father and Lindsay Wagner played Samson's mother.

Charles Robert Darwin was an English naturalist, geologist and biologist, best known for his contributions to the science of evolution. Darwin published his theory of evolution with compelling evidence in his 1859 book "On the Origin of Species". Darwin also published other books throughout his life including the following: "Geological Observations on the Volcanic Islands" in 1844; "The Descent of Man, and Selection in Relation to Sex" in 1871; "The Effects of Cross and Self Fertilisation in the Vegetable Kingdom" in 1876; and "The Different Forms of Flowers on Plants of the Same Species" in 1877.

Unmanned Aerial Vehicles or UAVs include both autonomous, capable of operating without human input, drones and remotely piloted vehicles or RPVs. A UAV is capable of controlled, sustained level flight and is powered by a jet, reciprocating, or electric engine. In the twenty first century, technology reached a point of sophistication that the UAV is now being given a greatly expanded role in many areas of aviation. The US Navy began experimenting with radio-controlled aircraft during the 1930s, resulting in the Curtiss N2C-2 drone in 1937. N2C-2 anti-aircraft target drones were in service by 1938.

There is more than one movie called Dog Days. Dog Days released March 8, 1925 is an Our Gang short. Dog Days released in September 2, 2001 is an Australian film which follows six interwoven stories set in suburban Vienna over the course of a summer weekend. Dog Days released August 8, 2018 is an American romantic comedy that follows the intertwining lives of various dogs and their owners around Los Angeles.

"Don't Worry, Be Happy", a song by American musician Bobby McFerrin, was released in September 1988. It was the first a cappella song to reach number-one on the Billboard Hot 100 chart, a position it held for two weeks. The lead single from Guy Sebastian's seventh album Armageddon, "Don't Worry, Be Happy", was released digitally in Australia on 18 November 2011.

In the 1993 American romantic comedy film Sleepless in Seattle, Tom Hanks plays protagonist Sam Baldwin, who lives in a houseboat at the address 2460 Westlake Avenue North.

Diving has been featured in many Commonwealth Games. The aquatics events at the 2010 Commonwealth Games took place at the SPM Swimming Pool Complex, Talkatora Gardens, Delhi from 4–13 October 2010. The diving competitions at the 2014 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland took place between 30 July and 2 August at the Royal Commonwealth Pool in Edinburgh, while the diving competitions at the 2018 Commonwealth Games in the Gold Coast, Queensland was held from 10 to 14 April at the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre.

St Patrick's Day parades began in North America in the 18th century but did not spread to Ireland until the 20th century. Although the first Irish St. Patrick's Day parade was held in Waterford in 1903, the first state-sponsored iteration of the celebration took place in Dublin in 1931. Internationally, St. Patrick's Day parades first took place in 1992 in Moscow, Russia, and Tokyo, Japan, as well as in 1976 in Itaewon and Daehangno, Seoul, South Korea.

Some states observe both the eastern time zone and the central time zone. In the Midwest, the Midway through Kentucky and Tennessee observe the eastern time zone change to central. In the south, midway through Florida also experience both the eastern time zone and the central time zone and in the north, between Michigan and Wisconsin, they observe their time zone change from eastern time zone to central.

The Bosnian War took place in 1992-95 in Bosnia and Herzegovina, a former Ottoman province that has historically been a multi-ethnic state. The war was a result of the breakup of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia. The United States supported the Bosnian Army through the CIA, and also supported the international organization, NATO, during the war.

Popular Christmas song "The Little Drummer Boy" was written by the American classical music composer and teacher Katherine Kennicott Davis in 1941. Davis published the song as "Czech Carol freely transcribed by K. K. D. ", although her initials were later replaced with her pseudonym C. R. W. Robinson. The version of the song most commonly sung today was arranged by Jack Halloran for Dot Records, which led to Dot producer Henry Onorati and his friend Harry Simeone releasing a near-identical version of the song for which they claimed and received joint composition credits with Davis, despite never composing, writing, or arranging the piece.

Australia came in 30th in the 2018 FIFA world cup in Group Stage level. In the 2015 Women's FIFA world cup, they made it to the Quarter-finals. In 2018, they came in 7th place. And, in the 2019 Women's FIFA world cup, they made it to Round of 16.

Australia came in 30th in the 2018 FIFA world cup in Group Stage level. In the 2015 Women's FIFA world cup, they made it to the Quarter-finals. In 2018, they came in 7th place. And, in the 2019 Women's FIFA world cup, they made it to Round of 16.

The name of the Samaritan woman at the well is Photine meaning "the luminous one". In Eastern Christian tradition, the woman's name at the time of her meeting Jesus is unknown, though she was later baptized "Photine".

Sosoliso Airlines Flight 1145 was a scheduled domestic passenger flight between the Nigerian cities Abuja and Port Harcourt that crash-landed on December 10, 2005. Out of 110 people on board the flight, seven people were recovered from the crash site and taken to hospitals, but only two survived their injuries. One of the survivors was Bunmi Amusan, a volunteer for Doctors Without Borders. The other was Kechi Okwuchi, a secondary school student at Ignatius Loyola Jesuit College, a boarding school in Abuja.

The Japanese science fiction neo-noir anime series Cowboy Bebop ran for a total of 26 episodes over 2 seasons from April 3 to June 26, 1998. Two related Cowboy Bebop manga series have since been released. The first manga series was Cowboy Bebop: Shooting Star, illustrated by Cain Kuga and released in 2 volumes in 1998. The second manga series was simply titled Cowboy Bebop, illustrated by Yutaka Nanten and released in 3 volumes in 1999 and 2000.

Understanding Poetry by Cleanth Brooks and Robert Penn Warren, first published in 1938, focuses on the study of poetry and encapsulates the principles of the New Criticism formalist literary theory movement. New Criticism dominated American literary criticism in the middle of the 20th century and emphasized close readings of poetry to discover how a work functioned as a self-contained, self-referential aesthetic object.

In 1884, the U. S. Congress first established a Bureau of Labor Statistics with the Bureau of Labor Act, to collect information about labor and employment. This bureau was under the Department of the Interior, and in 1888, the Bureau of Labor became an independent Department of Labor, but lacked executive rank. In February 1903, it became a bureau again when the Department of Commerce and Labor was established. United States President William Howard Taft signed the March 4, 1913, bill, the last day of his presidency, establishing the Department of Labor as a Cabinet-level department.

The Canal du Midi is a 240 km, 150 mi, long canal in Southern France connecting the Garonne to the Étang de Thau on the Mediterranean, and along with the 193 km, 120 mi, long Canal de Garonne forms the Canal des Deux Mers, joining the Atlantic to the Mediterranean. Strictly speaking, "Canal du Midi" refers to the portion initially constructed from Toulouse to the Mediterranean – the Deux-Mers canal project aimed to link together several sections of navigable waterways to join the Mediterranean and the Atlantic, first the Canal du Midi, then the Garonne which was more or less navigable between Toulouse and Bordeaux, then the Garonne Lateral Canal built later, and finally the Gironde estuary after Bordeaux.

The Bribery Act of 2010, an Act of Parliament covering the criminal law relating to bribery in the United Kingdom, was given Royal Assent on 8 April 2010 and came into effect on 1 July 2011. The Act has near-universal jurisdiction and has been described as "the toughest anti-corruption legislation in the world".

"Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas" is a 1943 song composed by Hugh Martin and written by Ralph Blane for the MGM musical "Meet Me in St. Louis". It was later covered by various artists and even ranked in 2007 by ASCAP as the third most performed Christmas song during the preceding five years.

A Low Down Dirty Shame is an American action comedy film written, directed by, and starring Keenen Ivory Wayans. The movie follows Andre Shame, a former LAPD detective turned private investigator in risk of being forced to close down his own firm. The movie was released on November 23, 1994, shortly after its soundtrack came out on November 8, 1994. The album contained hip hop and R&B music and peaked at #70 on the Billboard 200 and #14 on the Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums.
